Summary:
- publisher=UKMTO, title=JMIC Advisories - 2026, directness=high, why=JMIC advisories are the authoritative source for UKMTO’s commercial transit guidance in the Strait of Hormuz; the 2026 archive page is the primary location to verify May 2026 advisory status, next=https://www.ukmto.org/partner-products/jmic-products/jmic-advisories/2026  
- publisher=UKMTO, title=Advisories - 2026, directness=high, why=Confirms UKMTO’s 2026 advisories are hosted under /ukmto-products/advisories/2026; may contain overlapping or complementary content to JMIC page, next=https://www.ukmto.org/ukmto-products/advisories/2026  
- publisher=IMO, title=No safe transit through Strait of Hormuz: IMO Secretary-General, directness=high, why=Official IMO statement dated 24 April 2026 explicitly declares no safe transit, directly supporting the claim of impossibility in May 2026; critical corroborating evidence, next=https://www.imo.org/en/mediacentre/pressbriefings/pages/no-safe-transit-through-hormuz-imo-secretary-general.aspx  
- publisher=IMO, title=Highlighted (Confirmed) incidents, directness=high, why=As of 6 May 2026, 32 confirmed incidents and 10 seafarer fatalities in the Strait of Hormuz provide operational context for transit impossibility, next=https://www.imo.org/en/mediacentre/hottopics/pages/middle-east-highlighted-incidents.aspx  
- publisher=IMO, title=Middle East Information, directness=medium, why=Confirms ongoing instability and IMO’s active monitoring of seafarers stranded in the region, reinforcing systemic risk, next=https://www.imo.org/en/mediacentre/hottopics/pages/middle-east-strait-of-hormuz.aspx  
- publisher=UKMTO, title=Monthly Reports, directness=medium, why=UKMTO’s Monthly Maritime Security Update Report may contain May 2026 transit analysis; verify if JMIC advisory is referenced or expanded upon, next=https://www.ukmto.org/ukmto-products/monthly-reports
